title: mTLS Certificate Rotation Tracking
dataSources: Certificate inventory scan (serial, subject, expiry), Istio/Linkerd cert rotation logs
spl:
index=certs (sourcetype="cert:inventory" OR sourcetype="istio:cert" OR sourcetype="linkerd:cert")
| eval expiry_epoch=if(isnum(expiry_time), expiry_time, strptime(expiry_time, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"))
| eval days_to_expiry=floor((expiry_epoch - now())/86400)
| where days_to_expiry < 60 OR days_to_expiry < 0
| eval status=case(days_to_expiry < 0, "EXPIRED", days_to_expiry < 14, "CRITICAL", days_to_expiry < 30, "WARNING", 1=1, "OK")
| table _time, subject, serial, expiry_epoch, days_to_expiry, status, workload, namespace
| sort days_to_expiry
description: Mutual TLS certificates approaching expiry in zero-trust architectures (service-to-service auth). Expired certs cause service outages and authentication failures.
value: PKI and platform teams track mTLS certificate lifecycles across services and infrastructure, catching expiring certificates before they cause authentication failures in zero-trust service-to-service communication.
implementation: Run periodic certificate inventory scans (OpenSSL, cert-manager, HashiCorp Vault) and forward to Splunk. Ingest Istio/Linkerd cert rotation logs for service mesh. Parse subject, serial, notAfter. Alert when cert expires in <30 days; critical alert at <14 days. Track rotation success/failure from mesh logs. Report on cert distribution by workload and expiry timeline. Integrate with automation for cert renewal.
visualization: Table (certs expiring soon), Single value (expired count), Bar chart (expiry by month), Timeline (rotation events).
cimSpl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic
  by All_Traffic.user, All_Traffic.action, All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest span=1h
| sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Anomalies from new SaaS app rollouts, browser updates, or vendor URL category changes.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Certificate inventory data in Splunk from automated scanning (e.g., cert-manager, Venafi, custom scripts) or service mesh logs (Istio, Linkerd).
• Data in `index=certs` with sourcetypes like `cert:inventory`, `istio:cert`, or `linkerd:cert`. Key fields: `serial_number`, `subject`, `issuer`, `not_before`, `not_after` (expiry), `hostname`, `service`, `rotation_event`.
• mTLS (mutual TLS) certificates are used for service-to-service authentication in zero-trust architectures. Unlike server certificates, mTLS certs are on both client and server, doubling the rotation surface.
• Create a `cert_policy.csv` lookup: `issuer_pattern,max_lifetime_days,rotation_window_days,owner`.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Verify certificate inventory:
```spl
index=certs (sourcetype="cert:inventory" OR sourcetype="istio:cert" OR sourcetype="linkerd:cert") earliest=-4h
| stats dc(serial_number) as certs dc(hostname) as hosts by issuer
```

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

Primary search — Certificate expiry tracking:
```spl
index=certs sourcetype="cert:inventory" earliest=-4h
| eval expiry_epoch=if(isnum(not_after), not_after, strptime(not_after, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S"))
| eval days_to_expiry=round((expiry_epoch - now())/86400, 0)
| eval urgency=case(days_to_expiry<0, "EXPIRED", days_to_expiry<=7, "CRITICAL", days_to_expiry<=30, "WARNING", days_to_expiry<=90, "PLAN", 1==1, "OK")
| where urgency IN ("EXPIRED", "CRITICAL", "WARNING")
| lookup cert_policy.csv issuer_pattern as issuer OUTPUT max_lifetime_days owner
| table hostname, service, subject, issuer, days_to_expiry, urgency, owner
| sort urgency, days_to_expiry
```

Understanding this SPL: Calculates days to expiry for all tracked certificates and flags those approaching or past expiration. In a service mesh (Istio), mTLS certificates auto-rotate (default 24h). If a cert is not rotating, the sidecar may be broken. For manually managed certs, 30-day warning gives time for renewal.

Rotation velocity:
```spl
index=certs sourcetype="cert:inventory" earliest=-7d
| where isnotnull(rotation_event)
| timechart span=1d dc(serial_number) as rotations dc(hostname) as hosts_rotated
```

Step 3 — Validate
(a) Check a certificate directly: `openssl s_client -connect <host>:443 | openssl x509 -noout -dates` and compare with Splunk.
(b) For Istio: `istioctl proxy-config secret <pod>` shows the current cert details.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard: Row 1 — "Expiring certs (<30d)", "Expired certs", "Rotation rate". Row 2 — Table: certs by urgency.
Alert: Immediate for expired or <7 day certs. Weekly for <30 day.

Runbook:
1. **Expired cert** — Emergency renewal. The service is likely failing mTLS handshakes.
2. **Cert not rotating in mesh** — Restart the sidecar proxy. Check cert-manager health.
3. **Lifetime exceeding policy** — Certificate was issued with too long a validity. Reissue.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **No cert data** — Automated scanning not configured. Set up a periodic script or cert-manager.
• **Expiry dates wrong** — Timezone or format parsing issue. Verify `strptime` format matches the data.
grandmaExplanation: We keep track of the digital certificates that let our computers prove their identity to each other, making sure none of them expire and cause connections to break.
